main.description: A named area an organization monitors over time (e.g. a mangrove plot that is re-flown by drone). The group holds no member list: layers join it by setting their `groupRef` to this record's AT-URI, so adding a new capture never edits the group. Clients derive semantics from the members — two or more distinct `capturedAt` days make the group a time series (time slider), and members sharing a capture day are products of the same survey (e.g. an orthomosaic plus its tree delineations) shown together at that stop.

name.description: Display name of the monitored area (e.g. 'Tumanan')

description.description: What this area is and why it is monitored

siteRef.description: AT-URI of the app.certified.location (project site) this area belongs to

bounds.description: Geographic footprint as 'west,south,east,north' in WGS84 decimal degrees. Optional — clients fall back to the union of member layer bounds.
